evidence: QueryExecutor interface defines a clean seam between the API layer and backend implementations, with RelationType enum collocating pattern knowledge in one place
Collocation and CollocationEntry records are minimal, explicit boundary types between the indexer and query layers
PosTagger interface provides a narrow, predictable handoff (sentence → List<TaggedToken>) that is honoured by all tagger implementations
ConcordanceResult has 5 constructors with shifting null fields — lemma, tag, word, rawXml are all null depending on which constructor path was used, making the query→API boundary unpredictable

BlackLabQueryExecutor has 5 overlapping private regex-based XML extraction helpers (extractLemmaFromMatch, extractCollocateFromMatch, extractDependentLemmaFromGroup, extractTokenFromSnippet, extractPosFromGroup) that all parse the same BlackLab match text with slightly different logic — concentrated glue-code entropy at the BlackLab→domain boundary

CQLVerifier exposes two nearly identical public methods — verify() and verifyForCollocate() — with the same implementation; a caller cannot determine which to use and the distinction is only a comment

POS-to-group mapping logic is scattered across three separate places with no composition: PosTagger.TaggedToken.getPosGroup() (Penn Treebank first-char switch), ConllUProcessor.mapUPOStoGroup() (UPOS switch), ConllUProcessor.guessUPOS() (PTB→UPOS heuristic); these three routines each implement an overlapping slice of the same mapping without referencing each other
CorpusProcessor.getTotalTokens() is a public API method that always returns 0, documented only as 'This would require tracking, but for now just return 0' — a broken contract at the module boundary

strengths: QueryExecutor interface provides a clean, explicit seam between the HTTP/API layer and all backend implementations, with RelationType enum placing multi-token pattern knowledge in one shared location
Collocation and CollocationEntry records are minimal, purpose-built data-transfer types — the handoff from indexer to query is explicit and has no hidden fields
PosTagger interface provides a narrow, predictable boundary contract (sentence text in, List<TaggedToken> out) that decouples the tagging pipeline from the corpus processing and indexing layers
QueryResults centralises all domain result types (WordSketchResult, ConcordanceResult) in one place, giving consumers a single import point for query outputs
mid_level_elegance.issue_character: Issues are concentrated at two concrete boundary implementations — the BlackLab→domain translation layer and the shared query result type — where ad-hoc parsing and constructor proliferation create unpredictable and redundant seams rather than isolated rough edges.
mid_level_elegance.score_rationale: The codebase has genuinely clean architectural seams at the interface level (PosTagger, QueryExecutor, Collocation records), but the concrete implementations that bridge external libraries to domain types show recurring glue-code entropy. The BlackLabQueryExecutor concentrates five overlapping XML-scraping helpers at one boundary, ConcordanceResult's five-constructor spread makes the query→API handoff non-deterministic for consumers, and POS mapping logic is triply scattered with no shared root. These are exactly the patterns the dimension targets — translation at boundaries happening in multiple places instead of one, and orchestration that back-and-forths through ad-hoc extraction rather than composing clean collaborators. This places the codebase in the 60–70 'mixed' range: the high-level seam design is solid, but the developer regularly encounters friction when crossing concrete module boundaries.

issues.summary: ConcordanceResult exposes 5 constructors with shifting nullable fields, making the query→API boundary unpredictable
evidence: ConcordanceResult has 5 constructors; lemma, tag, word, and rawXml are null in 3 of them, but callers receive the same type regardless of which path created the object
A consumer calling getSentence(), getLemma(), getTag() cannot know which fields are populated without knowing the construction context
The 'full constructor with raw XML' and the 'BlackLab-style results' constructor both populate sentence but leave lemma/tag/word null for different reasons
issues.suggestion: Split ConcordanceResult into two or three purpose-specific result types (e.g., TokenResult for lemma/tag/word-populated results and SnippetResult for concordance-style results), each with only the fields it guarantees to populate. Alternatively, use a sealed interface with distinct subtypes and eliminate the null-field ambiguity entirely.

issues.summary: BlackLabQueryExecutor has five overlapping regex-based XML extraction helpers at the BlackLab→domain boundary
evidence: extractLemmaFromMatch, extractCollocateFromMatch, extractDependentLemmaFromGroup, extractTokenFromSnippet, and extractPosFromGroup all parse the same BlackLab XML match string using the same LEMMA_ATTR/XPOS_ATTR/UPOS_ATTR patterns
extractCollocateFromMatch and extractTokenFromSnippet are functionally identical (nth regex match from XML) but are separate methods with different parameter names
The plain-text fallback in findCollocations (lastIndexOf(' ')) and extractCollocateFromPlainText in executeSurfacePattern implement the same fallback logic independently
issues.suggestion: Extract a single BlackLabMatchParser class that owns all match-text-to-domain translation. Expose methods like extractLemmaAtPosition(text, n), extractPos(text), extractLastLemma(text). Call this from all three findCollocations / executeSurfacePattern / findByDependencyRelation paths. This consolidates translation at one obvious place and eliminates the parallel partial implementations.

issues.summary: POS-to-group mapping is implemented three times across three files with no shared root
evidence: PosTagger.TaggedToken.getPosGroup() maps Penn Treebank first char (N→noun, V→verb, J→adj, R→adv, D→det, P→pron, I→prep, C→conj, U→punct)
ConllUProcessor.mapUPOStoGroup() maps Universal POS strings (NOUN/PROPN/PRON→noun, VERB→verb, ADJ→adj, AUX→verb, CCONJ/SCONJ→conj) with different groupings
ConllUProcessor.guessUPOS() converts Penn Treebank tags back to Universal POS using yet another first-char switch, creating a round-trip path that is inconsistent with TaggedToken.getPosGroup()
issues.suggestion: Introduce a single PosGroupMapper utility class (or static methods on a shared PosUtils class) with two clearly named factory methods: fromPennTreebank(String xpos) and fromUniversalPos(String upos). Both ConllUProcessor and TaggedToken should delegate to this shared class. This puts tag translation at one obvious place and eliminates the three diverging implementations.

issues.summary: CQLVerifier.verify() and verifyForCollocate() are nearly identical, creating a misleading dual seam
evidence: verify() and verifyForCollocate() have the same method body: build steps, call matchSequence, return VerificationResult
The only documented difference is the comment 'looking for collocates' and 'The headword is at position 0 (conceptually)' — there is no structural difference in the implementation
A caller invoking CQLVerifier cannot determine which method to call; the seam provides no meaningful distinction
issues.suggestion: Delete verifyForCollocate() and route all callers through verify(). If a headword-at-position-zero semantic is needed, make it explicit via a parameter (e.g., verify(pattern, window, headwordPosition)) which already exists, making the method signature self-explanatory.

issues.summary: CorpusProcessor.getTotalTokens() is a public API method that always returns 0
evidence: getTotalTokens() has the comment 'This would require tracking, but for now just return 0' and returns 0 unconditionally
processCorpus() does track totalTokens locally and logs it, but does not persist it to any field
Any caller relying on this method after processCorpus() receives a broken answer from an apparently complete module boundary
issues.suggestion: Add a private int totalTokensProcessed field to CorpusProcessor, increment it inside processSentenceBuffer(), and return it from getTotalTokens(). This is a single-edit fix that makes the API honest.

root_causes: boundary_translation_entropy: Multiple parallel ad-hoc translation helpers at external-library seams (BlackLab, POS taggers) instead of a single owned translation layer
boundary_type_sprawl: Shared result types accumulating overloaded constructors across evolution cycles rather than being split into purpose-specific types
